bird.name: 黑長腳鷸
bird.summary: 黑長腳鷸（學名：Himantopus novaezelandiae，毛利語：Kakī）是一種反嘴鹬科中的大形涉禽，是紐西蘭的特有種。其种加词“novaezealandiae”意为“新西兰的”。成鳥約40厘米長。牠們的足紅色而且相當長，並有一個黑色長而薄的喙及獨有的混身黑色的羽毛…

locations.name: 约翰·古尔德
locations.summary: 约翰·古尔德 FRS（英語：John Gould，1804年9月14日—1881年2月3日）是19世纪一位英国鸟类学家。1804年生于英格兰，也是一位商人、艺术家。1838年后与其妻一同前往澳大利亚，在当地发现并為许多新物种命名。 早年…

locations.name: 辮狀河流
locations.summary: 辮狀河流又稱辮狀河、網狀河、交織河道、河流辫、辫状河道，是指河道网，這些河道網中的河道之间被小型或临时存在的岛屿分隔，这些岛屿被称为辫条。辮狀河流往往出现在泥沙量大或颗粒粗大的河流中，也出现在坡度较陡的河流中。辮狀河流在世界各地的各种自然…

locations.name: 霍克湾大区
locations.summary: 霍克湾（英語：Hawke's Bay；毛利語：Heretaunga；又譯霍克斯灣、豪克斯湾）是位於紐西蘭北島東岸的一個大區，地區議會設於內皮爾及哈斯丁（Hastings）兩市。 地理 豪克斯灣位於紐西蘭北島東岸，其名稱沿用了豪克灣（Haw…

locations.name: 坎特伯雷大区
locations.summary: 坎特伯雷（英語：Canterbury，毛利語：Waitaha）是位於紐西蘭南島的一個行政區域。主要包含紐西蘭最大平原坎特伯雷平原和周圍的山地。其主要城市基督城為其地方政府、坎特伯雷議會和坎特伯雷大學的所在地。 地理 坎特伯雷面積42,20…

locations.name: 菲奥德兰
locations.summary: 菲奥德兰（英語：Fiordland）是新西兰南岛西南角的一个地理区域，新西兰国内最大的国家公园──占地12,120平方千米的菲奥德兰国家公园覆盖该区的大部分面积。南阿尔卑斯山脉及其西面被海水淹浸的山谷形成了菲奥德兰的地理特征。区内的布朗瀑…

relations.subject: 黑長腳鷸
relations.evidence: The black stilt or kakī (Māori), (Himantopus novaezelandiae) is a wading bird found in New Zealand

relations.subject: 黑長腳鷸
relations.evidence: Although black stilts currently breed only in braided river systems in the South Island, subfossil bones show that prior to human arrival they were found in habitats as diverse as narrow bush streams in Hawkes Bay, and a wetland lake surrounded by forest in North Canterbury. In the nineteenth century they bred on riverbeds and in wetlands of the central and eastern North Island and most of the South Island except Fiordland

relations.subject: 黑長腳鷸
relations.evidence: Most black stilts will also overwinter in the Mackenzie Basin, but about 10% of the population, especially hybrids and those paired with pied stilts, migrate to North Island harbours such as Kawhia and Kaipara in January for the winter

relations.object_summary: 浅水、沼泽与植被斑块组成的高生产力生态系统。

relations.subject: 黑長腳鷸
relations.evidence: Black stilts rely on wetlands and braided riverbeds for feeding, and these have been extensively drained or modified for agriculture, irrigation, and flood control. Invasive weeds such as Russell lupin and crack willow are able to colonise braided riverbeds, reducing nesting habitat and providing cover for predators. Because black stilts nest on braided river beds, they are threatened by changes in river flows as a result of new and existing hydroelectric dams. As part of the Upper Waitaki Power Development, braided riverbeds in the Upper Waitaki were receiving less water, which allowed weeds to encroach into black stilt breeding areas
relations.object_summary: 浅水、沼泽与植被斑块组成的高生产力生态系统。

relations.subject: 黑長腳鷸
relations.evidence: Black stilts largely breed in the Mackenzie Basin in the South Island, and are threatened by introduced feral cats, ferrets, and hedgehogs as well as habitat degradation from hydroelectric dams, agriculture, and invasive weeds
relations.object_summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。
